body.title: Engram portability — extract, exchange, backup, restore
body.result: EngramTemplate + export_engram() + import_engram() + _scan_dependencies(). Dependency manifest catalogs tool_refs, memory_refs, channel_refs, env_vars. BFS subgraph extraction. 13 tests.
body.description:
Make engrams (habits, procedures, traversal programs) portable units that can be extracted, versioned, exchanged between Igors, backed up independently, and restored.

## Motivation
Engrams are woven into Igor's graph fabric, but they need to be:
- **Extractable**: pull an engram out as a self-contained unit (code + edges + metadata)
- **Versionable**: track edit history for rollback of failed self-experiments (version tree in metadata, not a new memory type)
- **Exchangeable**: Igors sharing patterns = sharing engrams. Must be easy to serialize, transfer, and graft into another Igor's graph
- **Backupable**: independent of full DB backup — back up just the engrams, or just a specific engram
- **Restorable**: graft a backed-up or received engram back into the graph with edges intact

## Design considerations
- Engram = subgraph (root node + children + interpretive edges + metadata). Extraction = serialize the subgraph.
- Exchange format: JSON or similar, self-contained with all edges and metadata. Receiver grafts it into their own graph.
- Version tree lives in metadata (per feedback: no new memory types, tags only)
- Clan schema is where shared engrams live — exchanged patterns go there
- Relates to calving: a calved subtree is structurally similar to an extracted engram

## Use cases
- Igor experiments with a habit edit → it degrades → rollback to previous version
- A new Igor boots and receives proven engrams from the clan
- Akien asks Igor to share a useful pattern with another instance
- Backup/restore specific high-value engrams without touching the rest of the DB

## Related
- T-versioned-engrams — versioning aspect (subset of this)
- T-versioned-memories — versioned flag in metadata (shared infrastructure)
- T-graph-calving-execution — calve_subtree is related extraction primitive
